title: Semiconductor ecosystem
subtitle: Who makes the world's chips, how big they are, and how they depend on each other.
financials_note: Five-year income-statement trends for the chokepoints. Revenue bars read on the left; margins read on the right. This is where the fabless-versus-fab economics and the memory cycle show up.
chokepoints_note: The companies the rest of the industry has the fewest alternatives to, ranked by market value. The bars show the fabless-versus-fab divide: who sells vs. who spends to build.
notes_placeholder: General notes on the semiconductor industry...

layers.label: Integrated device makers
layers.desc: Design and fabricate under one roof

chokepoint_why.Nvidia: Dominant designer of the AI accelerators the whole industry is racing to buy.
chokepoint_why.TSMC: Fabricates most of the world's leading-edge chips; almost no one can replace it.
chokepoint_why.ASML: The only company on Earth that makes EUV lithography machines.
chokepoint_why.Arm: Its instruction set sits inside almost every phone chip made.
chokepoint_why.Samsung: Vertically integrated across memory, foundry, components and finished devices.
chokepoint_why.Intel: A major integrated maker now opening its fabs to outside customers.
chokepoint_why.Applied Materials: The largest maker of the equipment that fabricates chips.
chokepoint_why.SK hynix: A key supplier of the HBM memory that AI accelerators depend on.
